description: Prettier must not touch XML; VS Code XML extension config for Salesforce metadata; how to suppress false-positive schema errors cleanly
metadata:
  node_type: memory
  type: feedback
  originSessionId: 8930ac2c-c768-45b2-9fcc-a7bcfdf57de3
---

## Prettier scope for Salesforce projects

**Never include `xml` in Prettier glob patterns.** Salesforce metadata files (permission sets, flows, objects, page layouts, etc.) are generated by the CLI or managed in the Salesforce UI. Prettier reformats them in ways that cause spurious diffs and breaks org ↔ source sync.

Prettier should only cover human-authored code:

```
cls, trigger       — Apex
html, js, css      — LWC
cmp, component, page — Visualforce/Aura
json, md           — config and docs
yaml, yml          — CI/tooling config
```

Remove `xml` from both `scripts.prettier` and `lint-staged` in `package.json`.

**Why:** Prettier reformatted description elements across multiple lines and reordered tab settings — diffs that had nothing to do with real changes and caused the org retrieve to show "Changed" on every commit.

---

## VS Code XML extension — suppressing Salesforce metadata false-positives

The correct settings (in `.vscode/settings.json`):

```json
"xml.validation.noGrammar": "ignore",
"xml.downloadExternalResources.enabled": false,
"xml.validation.filters": [
  { "pattern": "**/*-meta.xml", "enabled": false }
]
```

**Why all three:**

- `noGrammar: ignore` — suppresses errors when a file has no schema. Not enough alone because Salesforce metadata files declare `xmlns=...`, which the extension treats as "grammar found".
- `downloadExternalResources.enabled: false` — stops the extension fetching Salesforce XSDs from the namespace URL.
- `xml.validation.filters` — the definitive fix: disables validation entirely for all `-meta.xml` files. Takes effect immediately, no cache clear or window reload required. "XML: Clear Schema Cache" is not a reliable command — it may not exist depending on the extension version.

---

## Anti-patterns that made things worse

**`systemId: ""`** — Does NOT mean "no schema." It means "use an empty external DTD," which triggers DTD validation mode and flags every single element as undeclared. Result: 130 errors instead of 1. Never use `systemId: ""` to suppress validation.

**Local XSD with `targetNamespace`** — A local `.xsd` file (e.g. `sf-recordtype.xsd`) that declares `targetNamespace="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"` causes the XML extension to apply that namespace's cached schema to ALL files sharing that namespace — including permission sets and other unrelated metadata types. This caused `salesforce_metadata_api_common.xsd` (a broken cached Salesforce XSD with a `MetadataForSettings` type reference error) to surface as a false-positive on permission set files. Fix: remove the fileAssociation and rely on `noGrammar + downloadExternalResources` instead.

---

## Permission set deployment gotcha

Required fields (`<required>true</required>`) cannot be included in a permission set's `<fieldPermissions>`. Salesforce enforces this at deploy time with: _"You cannot deploy to a required field: Object.Field\_\_c"_. Simply omit required fields from the permission set — they are automatically accessible to anyone with object access.

**Why:** Required fields must always be writable; the permission system doesn't need to grant what's already guaranteed.
